name: Port del Compte - Ruta circular desde el Telesilla Sucre
region_intro: Mountain biking around La Coma i la Pedra offers diverse terrain within the Solsonès region of Lleida, Catalonia. The area features imposing mountains, including the Sierra del Port del Comte and Sierra del Verd, with an average altitude exceeding 2,000 meters. Landscapes vary from dense pine and fir forests to expansive alpine meadows and rocky areas. The Cardener River traverses the municipality, adding to the varied scenery for mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails around La Coma I La Pedra The most popular mountain bike trail is Port del Compte - Ruta circular desde el Telesilla Sucre, a 23.1 miles (37.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 30 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the mountainous area around Port del Comte. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Mirador de l'Art – Coll de Jou loop from Estació d'esquí nòrdic de Tuixent - La Vansa, a difficult 28.0 miles (45.0 km) path. This route features significant elevation gains and panoramic views from Mirador de l'Art. Local mountain bikers also love the The Capolatell (Busa Prison) – Sant Llorenç de Morunys loop from la Coma i la Pedra, a 34.1 miles (55.0 km) trail leading through varied mountainous terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 9 minutes. Mountain biking around La Coma i la Pedra is defined by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and alpine meadows. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes feature significant elevation gains. The routes in La Coma i la Pedra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore La Coma i la Pedra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in La Coma i la Pedra?
A: There are over 70 mountain bike trails in La Coma i la Pedra, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored more than 1300 mountain biking routes in the area.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels and terrain like for mountain biking in La Coma i la Pedra?
A: The mountain biking routes in La Coma i la Pedra are predominantly challenging, with 53 routes rated as difficult and 18 as moderate. The terrain is characterized by mountainous landscapes, dense forests of pines and firs, expansive alpine meadows, and rocky areas. Many routes feature significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in La Coma i la Pedra?
A: Yes, many routes in La Coma i la Pedra are designed as circular loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949650450/port-del-compte-ruta-circular-desde-el-telesilla-sucre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port del Compte - Ruta circular desde el Telesilla Sucre&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38276180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Capolatell (Busa Prison) – Sant Llorenç de Morunys loop from la Coma i la Pedra&lt;/a&gt; also offers a substantial circular ride.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see while mountain biking in the area?
A: The region offers stunning natural features. You can find routes that pass by the sources of the Cardener River, which are particularly impressive during snowmelt. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34341735" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de l'Art – Coll de Jou loop&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic views from Mirador de l'Art. The area is also surrounded by imposing mountains like the Sierra del Port del Comte and Sierra del Verd, offering extraordinary vistas. You can also explore peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4483750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bald Hen Peak&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5319396" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of La Mola de Lord (1,189 m)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites accessible by mountain bike?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore the charming old town of La Coma with its rehabilitated streets. The traditional stone architecture of La Pedra includes the Romanesque church of Sant Serni de la Pedra and the sulfidic waters of the Puda fountain, known for their medicinal properties. Some routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38276180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Capolatell (Busa Prison) – Sant Llorenç de Morunys loop&lt;/a&gt;, lead through areas with historical significance.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in La Coma i la Pedra?
A: La Coma i la Pedra offers diverse landscapes year-round. While the area is known for winter sports around Port del Comte, spring, summer, and autumn are ideal for mountain biking. During snowmelt, the Cardener River's sources offer a majestic spectacle, making spring a particularly picturesque time.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can find routes that incorporate natural water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38279688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Llorenç de Morunys – La Coma Waterfall loop from la Coma i la Pedra&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route specifically designed to visit a waterfall near La Coma.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in La Coma i la Pedra?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse and striking natural landscape, the challenging mountainous terrain, and the varied scenery ranging from dense forests to expansive alpine meadows.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in La Coma i la Pedra?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed for every trailhead, areas like the Port del Comte ski resort often serve as starting points for various mountain biking trails and typically offer parking facilities. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions for recommended starting points and parking.

Q: How can I reach La Coma i la Pedra using public transport for mountain biking?
A: Information regarding direct public transport options specifically for mountain bikers to La Coma i la Pedra is limited. The region is more accessible by private vehicle, which allows for greater flexibility in reaching various trailheads. It is recommended to plan your journey accordingly.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails suitable for those looking for a less strenuous ride?
A: Yes, while many routes are difficult, there are 18 moderate mountain bike trails available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34341269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Jou Churro Shop – Sant Llorenç de Morunys loop from la Coma&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a good balance of challenge and accessibility.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that explore the Port del Comte area?
A: Absolutely. The area around the Port del Comte ski resort is a popular starting point for mountain biking. A notable route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949650450/port-del-compte-ruta-circular-desde-el-telesilla-sucre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port del Compte - Ruta circular desde el Telesilla Sucre&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the mountainous terrain of this region.
tips: Highest point of the Port del Comte massif. This peak bears this name because it is a historical dividing point between four municipalities.
A stony ridge of 4.3 km in length that, from the top of the Quatre Batlles, ends in La Collada de l'Estany at 1784 meters.
Near the Colado del Estany, we will find this beautiful print that gives us the small pond of Pratmajor, located in the Sierra de Campelles.
Also, Llano de la Bófia. Located at an altitude of about 2,000 meters, it is perfect for resting and enjoying nature in a quiet setting of subalpine meadows.
Viewpoint with views of Montserrat and the Lord's Sanctuary, like the homonymous Valley.
